channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: When Prompt Optimization Becomes Jailbreaking: Adaptive Red-Teaming of Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.19247v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into high-stakes applications, making robust safety guarantees a central practical and commercial concern. Existing safety evaluations predominantly rely on fixed collections of harmful prompts,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, RAG, large language model, prompt, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Spelling Correction in Healthcare Query-Answer Systems: Methods, Retrieval Impact, and Empirical Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.19249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Healthcare question-answering (QA) systems face a persistent challenge: users submit queries with spelling errors at rates substantially higher than those found in the professional documents they search. This paper presents the first controlled study ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, experiment, study, retrieval, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Enhancing Legal LLMs through Metadata-Enriched RAG Pipelines and Direct Preference Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2603.19251v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) perform well in short contexts but degrade on long legal documents, often producing hallucinations such as incorrect clauses or precedents. In the legal domain, where precision is critical, such errors undermine reliabilit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, LLM, retrieval, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GeoChallenge: A Multi-Answer Multiple-Choice Benchmark for Geometric Reasoning with Diagrams
item.description: arXiv:2603.19252v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating the symbolic reasoning of large language models (LLMs) calls for geometry benchmarks that require multi-step proofs grounded in both text and diagrams. However, existing benchmarks are often limited in scale and rarely provide visually grou...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, large language model, reasoning, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A comprehensive study of LLM-based argument classification: from Llama through DeepSeek to GPT-5.2
item.description: arXiv:2603.19253v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Argument mining (AM) is an interdisciplinary research field focused on the automatic identification and classification of argumentative components, such as claims and premises, and the relationships between them. Recent advances in large language mode...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, large language model, prompt, LLM, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Comprehension to Reasoning: A Hierarchical Benchmark for Automated Financial Research Reporting
item.description: arXiv:2603.19254v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate financial research reports, shifting from auxiliary analytic tools to primary content producers. Yet recent real-world deployments reveal persistent failures--factual errors, numerical inc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, large language model, reasoning, LLM, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LARFT: Closing the Cognition-Action Gap for Length Instruction Following in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.19255v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the strong performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) on complex instruction-following tasks, precise control of output length remains a persistent challenge. Existing methods primarily attempt to enforce length constraints by externally impo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning, large language model, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ShobdoSetu: A Data-Centric Framework for Bengali Long-Form Speech Recognition and Speaker Diarization
item.description: arXiv:2603.19256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Bengali is spoken by over 230 million people yet remains severely under-served in automatic speech recognition (ASR) and speaker diarization research. In this paper, we present our system for the DL Sprint 4.0 Bengali Long-Form Speech Recognition (Tas...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, paper, LLM, research, audio | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hyperagents
item.description: arXiv:2603.19461v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-improving AI systems aim to reduce reliance on human engineering by learning to improve their own learning and problem-solving processes. Existing approaches to self-improvement rely on fixed, handcrafted meta-level mechanisms, fundamentally limi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, memory, alignment, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Teaching an Agent to Sketch One Part at a Time
item.description: arXiv:2603.19500v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We develop a method for producing vector sketches one part at a time. To do this, we train a multi-modal language model-based agent using a novel multi-turn process-reward reinforcement learning following supervised fine-tuning. Our approach is enable...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, arxiv, vector, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning to Disprove: Formal Counterexample Generation with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.19514v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mathematical reasoning demands two critical, complementary skills: constructing rigorous proofs for true statements and discovering counterexamples that disprove false ones. However, current AI efforts in mathematics focus almost exclusively on proof ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, paper, large language model, reasoning, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ItinBench: Benchmarking Planning Across Multiple Cognitive Dimensions with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.19515v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) with advanced cognitive capabilities are emerging as agents for various reasoning and planning tasks. Traditional evaluations often focus on specific reasoning or planning questions within controlled environments. Recent s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, large language model, reasoning, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PA2D-MORL: Pareto Ascent Directional Decomposition based Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.19579v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-objective reinforcement learning (MORL) provides an effective solution for decision-making problems involving conflicting objectives. However, achieving high-quality approximations to the Pareto policy set remains challenging, especially in comp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning, paper, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PowerLens: Taming LLM Agents for Safe and Personalized Mobile Power Management
item.description: arXiv:2603.19584v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Battery life remains a critical challenge for mobile devices, yet existing power management mechanisms rely on static rules or coarse-grained heuristics that ignore user activities and personal preferences. We present PowerLens, a system that tames th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, memory, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HyEvo: Self-Evolving Hybrid Agentic Workflows for Efficient Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.19639v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although agentic workflows have demonstrated strong potential for solving complex tasks, existing automated generation methods remain inefficient and underperform, as they rely on predefined operator libraries and homogeneous LLM-only workflows in whi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, reasoning, LLM, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Subgoal-driven Framework for Improving Long-Horizon LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.19685v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based agents have emerged as powerful autonomous controllers for digital environments, including mobile interfaces, operating systems, and web browsers. Web navigation, for example, requires handling dynamic content and long...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, RAG, fine-tuning, large language model, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Stepwise: Neuro-Symbolic Proof Search for Automated Systems Verification
item.description: arXiv:2603.19715v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Formal verification via interactive theorem proving is increasingly used to ensure the correctness of critical systems, yet constructing large proof scripts remains highly manual and limits scalability. Advances in large language models (LLMs), especi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, large language model, reasoning, LLM, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Speculating Experts Accelerates Inference for Mixture-of-Experts
item.description: arXiv:2603.19289v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have gained popularity as a means of scaling the capacity of large language models (LLMs) while maintaining sparse activations and reduced per-token compute. However, in memory-constrained inference settings, expert wei...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, LLM, memory, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Maximizing mutual information between user-contexts and responses improve LLM personalization with no additional data
item.description: arXiv:2603.19294v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While post-training has successfully improved large language models (LLMs) across a variety of domains, these gains heavily rely on human-labeled data or external verifiers. Existing data has already been exploited, and new high-quality data is expens...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, large language model, prompt, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CLaRE-ty Amid Chaos: Quantifying Representational Entanglement to Predict Ripple Effects in LLM Editing
item.description: arXiv:2603.19297v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The static knowledge representations of large language models (LLMs) inevitably become outdated or incorrect over time. While model-editing techniques offer a promising solution by modifying a model's factual associations, they often produce unpredict...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, LLM, memory, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Dynamic Bayesian and Machine Learning Framework for Quantitative Evaluation and Prediction of Operator Situation Awareness in Nuclear Power Plants
item.description: arXiv:2603.19298v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Operator situation awareness is a pivotal yet elusive determinant of human reliability in complex nuclear control environments. Existing assessment methods, such as SAGAT and SART, remain static, retrospective, and detached from the evolving cognitive...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, reasoning, study, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PRIME-CVD: A Parametrically Rendered Informatics Medical Environment for Education in Cardiovascular Risk Modelling
item.description: arXiv:2603.19299v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In recent years, progress in medical informatics and machine learning has been accelerated by the availability of openly accessible benchmark datasets. However, patient-level electronic medical record (EMR) data are rarely available for teaching or me...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, instruction, research, release, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Exploring Subnetwork Interactions in Heterogeneous Brain Network via Prior-Informed Graph Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.19307v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modeling the complex interactions among functional subnetworks is crucial for the diagnosis of mental disorders and the identification of functional pathways. However, learning the interactions of the underlying subnetworks remains a significant chall...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, arxiv, framework, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, context, prompt engineering, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, reasoning, audio, CoT, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, RAG, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, vector, reasoning, LLM, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, vector, prompt, LLM, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: DuCCAE: A Hybrid Engine for Immersive Conversation via Collaboration, Augmentation, and Evolution
item.description: arXiv:2603.19248v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Immersive conversational systems in production face a persistent trade-off between responsiveness and long-horizon task capability. Real-time interaction is achievable for lightweight turns, but requests involving planning and tool invocation (e.g., s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, experiment, tool, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Visualization for Comparative Analysis of Regression Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.19291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As regression is a widely studied problem, many methods have been proposed to solve it, each of them often requiring setting different hyper-parameters. Therefore, selecting the proper method for a given application may be very difficult and relies on...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, arxiv, ICL, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BrainSCL: Subtype-Guided Contrastive Learning for Brain Disorder Diagnosis
item.description: arXiv:2603.19295v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mental disorder populations exhibit pronounced heterogeneity -- that is, the significant differences between samples -- poses a significant challenge to the definition of positive pairs in contrastive learning. To address this, we propose a subtype-gu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, framework, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TTQ: Activation-Aware Test-Time Quantization to Accelerate LLM Inference On The Fly
item.description: arXiv:2603.19296v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: To tackle the huge computational demand of large foundation models, activation-aware compression techniques without retraining have been introduced. However, since these methods highly rely on calibration data, domain shift issues may arise for unseen...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, prompt, LLM, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Parameter-Efficient Token Embedding Editing for Clinical Class-Level Unlearning
item.description: arXiv:2603.19302v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine unlearning is increasingly important for clinical language models, where privacy regulations and institutional policies may require removing sensitive information from deployed systems without retraining from scratch. In practice, deletion req...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, embedding, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, model, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Can Structural Cues Save LLMs? Evaluating Language Models in Massive Document Streams
item.description: arXiv:2603.19250v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating language models in streaming environments is critical, yet underexplored. Existing benchmarks either focus on single complex events or provide curated inputs for each query, and do not evaluate models under the conflicts that arise when mul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, summarization, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, prompt, step-by-step, example, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
